titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the De Forest Family Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by William E. Brown, Jr.

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 09:37

did.abstract: The papers consist of correspondence, account books, journals, letterbooks, financial records, and related papers of David Curtis De Forest and family members. The papers document the business and political careers of De Forest, including his commercial enterprise in Buenos Aires, Argentina. Correspondence, located within journals and letterbooks, includes letters written to family members, colleagues, and associates while on sea voyages to South America and elsewhere.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: De Forest Family Papers (MS 177). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Frederick Hill, 1939, and gift of Clementine De Forest Griffin, 1940.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in four series: I. David Curtis De Forest, 1798-1825. II. William B. De Forest, 1861-1873. III. George Butler Griffin, 1857-1889. IV. Family Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers consist of the account books, journals, letterbooks, financial records, and related papers of David Curtis De Forest (1774-1825), William B. De Forest, George Butler Griffin, and other family members. David Curtis De Forest files include documentation on his service as United States consul general to Argentina.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: David Curtis De Forest was born January 10, 1774 in Huntington, Connecticut. He worked on sailing vessels as a young man and served as a lieutenant in the army (1799-1801). De Forest established the first American commercial house in Buenos Aires, Argentina. Political and military unrest in Argentina (1811-1817) did not harm his business, and De Forest was commissioned as consul general to the United States by the new Argentine Republic (1815). De Forest resigned this position before receiving any U.S. diplomatic papers. De Forest died on February 22, 1825 in New Haven, Connecticut.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown, though much of the material in this collection is likely in the public domain. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains material arranged alphabetically by record type, with "Argentine financial records" organized at the end of the series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series is arranged alphabetically by record type.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series is arranged alphabetically by record type.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains material arranged alphabetically by the name of the individual. Miscellaneous items are filed at the end of the series.
